title: libei integrations in the XDG RemoteDesktop and InputCapture portals
excerpt: Turns out it's been years since I've talked about eggs, so let's change this. libei is, of course, the library for Emulated Input [1]. ...
content_html:
Turns out it's been years since I've talked about eggs, so let's change this. libei is, of course, the library for Emulated Input[1].

This post is mostly a refresher because it's been so long and a short summary of some of the work we've done so far, in preparation for some more posts that come soon.

libei is a transport layer for logical input events, unlike libinput which is a hardware abstraction layer. In libinput's case the device's firmare/kernel pass events that are somewhere on the sanity spectrum, libinput tries to make sense of those and then we convert those to logical events to be consumed by the next layer (typically the Wayland compositor or Xorg). This is how e.g. "touch down at position x1/y1, touch up at position x1/y2" is converted into a button click event if touchpad tapping is enabled. Or maybe into nothing if we find it was an accidental palm touch.

libei works purely on the logical level - you as the libei client pass logical events to the EIS (Emulated Input Server) implementation (typically the compositor). No guesswork, you say button click, EIS gets a button click. libei supports a "sender" and "receiver" mode, depending on whether events are sent to the EIS implementation (input emulation) or receive from the EIS implementation (input capture). libei is designed for the Wayland stack but there are zero requirements for Wayland on either the client or the EIS implementation.

Core to libei's design is that the EIS implementation is in control of virtually everything, it decides which devices are available to the client, when those devices can send events, etc. Much like the compositor is in charge when it comes to physical devices - if a compositor decides a physical device doesn't exist, a Wayland client cannot get events from it.

Since the original proposal (again, [1]!) we've been busy bees and libei is now a part of the XDG Remote Desktop portal and the XDG Input Capture (both since version 1.17, mid 2023). In both cases the portal is for the negotiation and initial agreement of what should happen, libei is then used as the transport layer between the two processes [2].

More recently we also added session persistence support so you don't have to allow access on every connecton. Much of the work enabling this was done by Jonas Ådahl, it is now in the portals since version 1.21.0 and should be in the major compositors in the current or next versions.

Plumbing the Pipes

Getting all this into place was a huge amount of work across several pieces of the stack. This isn't exciting in the same way as laying plumbing pipes isn't particularly exciting but much like regular plumbing: once it's in place you can change your diet without severely impacting everyone again. Try get that analogy out of your head now. You're welcome.

In libei's case this means three things:

  • if you have a client that uses the XDG portals to send/receive events they will now work with any compositor that implements the portal. No need for GNOME/KDE/... specific APIs.
  • if you have a compositor that implements EIS you have all the infrastructure in place to talk to libei clients from somewhere else, if need be. The use-cases for this aren't fully scoped yet (assisitive technologies, virtual keyboards, touchpads, etc?) but the piping is there and ready to be (ab)used .
  • since the actual events back and forth don't affect the layers in between, we can now add new events to libei without having to change everything else again.

Let's look at how this works in practice.

The XWayland XTEST use-case

An example for such a case where we can now abuse the piping is Xwayland support for XTEST. XTEST is the protocol that everyone uses to emulate input under X but in Wayland it's not hooked up to anything so those APIs simply won't work.

But what we can do in Xwayland is translate XTEST to libei events and facilitate the portal interaction. This means our stack looks roughly like this: 

    +--------------------+             +------------------+
    | Wayland compositor |---wayland---| Wayland client B |
    +--------------------+\            +------------------+
    | libinput |   EIS   | \_wayland______
    +----------+---------+                \
        |          |           +-------+------------------+
 /dev/input/       +-----------| libei |     XWayland     |
                               +-------+------------------+
                                                |
                                                | XTEST
                                                |
                                         +-----------+
                                         |  X client |
                                         +-----------+

And if said X client uses XTEST to try to emulate devices, Xwayland will ask the Remote Desktop portal for permission and set up the session, then pass the XTEST events on as libei events and voila - your 20 year old X client can send pointer and keyboard events through an XDG Portal without knowing about it (and the user can prohibit this and even gets some information on who is sending events which is not possible with normal XTEST at all). This has now been supported since Xwayland 23.2.0. Compositors don't need extra support for this.

What's next

So we have a lot of the plumbing in place, or in another anology: we have a hammer, let's go looking for nails. And right now the nails we can see are sending text, gestures, and tablet support. And those will be the subject of the next few posts.

[1]: 6 years ago?! whoah...
[2]: in Remote Desktop's case replacing the DBus emulation APIs which were a Newton's Cradle of wakeups for at least 4 processes per event

blocks.html_en: Turns out it's been years since I've talked about eggs, so let's change this. libei is, of course, the library for Emulated Input[1].

sentences.zh: 事实证明我已经好几年没有谈论鸡蛋了，所以让我们改变一下这一点。

sentences.en: libei is, of course, the library for Emulated Input[1].
sentences.zh: libei 当然是模拟输入的库[1]。

blocks.html_en: This post is mostly a refresher because it's been so long and a short summary of some of the work we've done so far, in preparation for some more posts that come soon.

sentences.en: This post is mostly a refresher because it's been so long and a short summary of some of the work we've done so far, in preparation for some more posts that come soon.
sentences.zh: 这篇文章主要是回顾一下，因为它已经很长了，并且是我们迄今为止所做的一些工作的简短总结，为即将发布的更多文章做准备。

blocks.html_en: libei is a transport layer for logical input events, unlike libinput which is a hardware abstraction layer. In libinput's case the device's firmare/kernel pass events that are somewhere on the sanity spectrum, libinput tries to make sense of those and then we convert those to logical events to be consumed by the next layer (typically the Wayland compositor or Xorg). This is how e.g. "touch down at position x1/y1, touch up at position x1/y2" is converted into a button click event if touchpad tapping is enabled. Or maybe into nothing if we find it was an accidental palm touch.

sentences.en: libei is a transport layer for logical input events, unlike libinput which is a hardware abstraction layer.
sentences.zh: libei 是逻辑输入事件的传输层，与硬件抽象层 libinput 不同。

sentences.en: In libinput's case the device's firmare/kernel pass events that are somewhere on the sanity spectrum, libinput tries to make sense of those and then we convert those to logical events to be consumed by the next layer (typically the Wayland compositor or Xorg).
sentences.zh: 在 libinput 的情况下，设备的固件/内核传递位于理智范围内的事件，libinput 尝试理解这些事件，然后我们将它们转换为逻辑事件以供下一层（通常是 Wayland 合成器或 Xorg）使用。

sentences.en: This is how e.g. "touch down at position x1/y1, touch up at position x1/y2" is converted into a button click event if touchpad tapping is enabled.
sentences.zh: 这就是例如如果启用了触摸板点击，“在位置 x1/y1 处触摸，在位置 x1/y2 触摸向上”将转换为按钮单击事件。

sentences.en: Or maybe into nothing if we find it was an accidental palm touch.
sentences.zh: 或者，如果我们发现这是一次意外的手掌触摸，则可能会一事无成。

blocks.html_en: libei works purely on the logical level - you as the libei client pass logical events to the EIS (Emulated Input Server) implementation (typically the compositor). No guesswork, you say button click, EIS gets a button click. libei supports a "sender" and "receiver" mode, depending on whether events are sent to the EIS implementation (input emulation) or receive from the EIS implementation (input capture). libei is designed for the Wayland stack but there are zero requirements for Wayland on either the client or the EIS implementation.

sentences.en: libei works purely on the logical level - you as the libei client pass logical events to the EIS (Emulated Input Server) implementation (typically the compositor).
sentences.zh: libei 纯粹在逻辑级别上工作 - 作为 libei 客户端，您将逻辑事件传递给 EIS（模拟输入服务器）实现（通常是合成器）。

sentences.en: No guesswork, you say button click, EIS gets a button click.
sentences.zh: 不用猜测，您说单击按钮，EIS 就会单击按钮。

sentences.en: libei supports a "sender" and "receiver" mode, depending on whether events are sent to the EIS implementation (input emulation) or receive from the EIS implementation (input capture).
sentences.zh: libei 支持“发送者”和“接收者”模式，具体取决于事件是发送到 EIS 实现（输入仿真）还是从 EIS 实现接收（输入捕获）。

sentences.en: libei is designed for the Wayland stack but there are zero requirements for Wayland on either the client or the EIS implementation.
sentences.zh: libei 是为 Wayland 堆栈设计的，但 Wayland 对客户端或 EIS 实现的要求为零。

blocks.html_en: Core to libei's design is that the EIS implementation is in control of virtually everything, it decides which devices are available to the client, when those devices can send events, etc. Much like the compositor is in charge when it comes to physical devices - if a compositor decides a physical device doesn't exist, a Wayland client cannot get events from it.

sentences.en: Core to libei's design is that the EIS implementation is in control of virtually everything, it decides which devices are available to the client, when those devices can send events, etc. Much like the compositor is in charge when it comes to physical devices - if a compositor decides a physical device doesn't exist, a Wayland client cannot get events from it.
sentences.zh: libei 设计的核心是 EIS 实现几乎控制一切，它决定哪些设备可供客户端使用、这些设备何时可以发送事件等。就像合成器在物理设备方面负责一样 - 如果合成器确定物理设备不存在，Wayland 客户端就无法从中获取事件。

blocks.html_en: Since the original proposal (again, [1]!) we've been busy bees and libei is now a part of the XDG Remote Desktop portal and the XDG Input Capture (both since version 1.17, mid 2023). In both cases the portal is for the negotiation and initial agreement of what should happen, libei is then used as the transport layer between the two processes [2].

sentences.en: Since the original proposal (again, [1]!)
sentences.zh: 自最初的提案以来（再次，[1]！）

sentences.en: we've been busy bees and libei is now a part of the XDG Remote Desktop portal and the XDG Input Capture (both since version 1.17, mid 2023).
sentences.zh: 我们一直很忙，libei 现在是 XDG 远程桌面门户和 XDG 输入捕获的一部分（均自 2023 年中期版本 1.17 起）。

sentences.en: In both cases the portal is for the negotiation and initial agreement of what should happen, libei is then used as the transport layer between the two processes [2].
sentences.zh: 在这两种情况下，门户都是用于协商并就应该发生的事情达成初步协议，然后使用 libei 作为两个进程之间的传输层 [2]。

blocks.html_en: More recently we also added session persistence support so you don't have to allow access on every connecton. Much of the work enabling this was done by Jonas Ådahl, it is now in the portals since version 1.21.0 and should be in the major compositors in the current or next versions.

sentences.en: More recently we also added session persistence support so you don't have to allow access on every connecton.
sentences.zh: 最近，我们还添加了会话持久性支持，因此您不必允许每个连接上的访问。

sentences.en: Much of the work enabling this was done by Jonas Ådahl, it is now in the portals since version 1.21.0 and should be in the major compositors in the current or next versions.
sentences.zh: 实现此功能的大部分工作是由 Jonas Ådahl 完成的，自版本 1.21.0 以来，它现已出现在门户中，并且应该出现在当前或下一版本的主要合成器中。

blocks.html_en: Plumbing the Pipes

sentences.en: Plumbing the Pipes
sentences.zh: 管道安装

blocks.html_en: Getting all this into place was a huge amount of work across several pieces of the stack. This isn't exciting in the same way as laying plumbing pipes isn't particularly exciting but much like regular plumbing: once it's in place you can change your diet without severely impacting everyone again. Try get that analogy out of your head now. You're welcome.

sentences.en: Getting all this into place was a huge amount of work across several pieces of the stack.
sentences.zh: 将所有这些都落实到位需要跨堆栈的多个部分进行大量工作。

sentences.en: This isn't exciting in the same way as laying plumbing pipes isn't particularly exciting but much like regular plumbing: once it's in place you can change your diet without severely impacting everyone again.
sentences.zh: 这并不令人兴奋，就像铺设管道也不是特别令人兴奋一样，但很像普通的管道：一旦安装到位，您就可以改变饮食，而不会再次严重影响每个人。

sentences.en: Try get that analogy out of your head now.
sentences.zh: 现在试着把这个比喻从你的脑海中抹去。

sentences.en: You're welcome.
sentences.zh: 不客气。

blocks.html_en: In libei's case this means three things:

sentences.en: In libei's case this means three things:
sentences.zh: 对于 libei 来说，这意味着三件事：

blocks.html_en: • if you have a client that uses the XDG portals to send/receive events they will now work with any compositor that implements the portal. No need for GNOME/KDE/... specific APIs.

sentences.en: if you have a client that uses the XDG portals to send/receive events they will now work with any compositor that implements the portal.
sentences.zh: 如果您有一个使用 XDG 门户发送/接收事件的客户端，它们现在将与实现该门户的任何合成器一起使用。

sentences.en: No need for GNOME/KDE/...
sentences.zh: 不需要 GNOME/KDE/...

sentences.en: specific APIs.
sentences.zh: 特定的 API。

blocks.html_en: • if you have a compositor that implements EIS you have all the infrastructure in place to talk to libei clients from somewhere else, if need be. The use-cases for this aren't fully scoped yet (assisitive technologies, virtual keyboards, touchpads, etc?) but the piping is there and ready to be (ab)used .

sentences.en: if you have a compositor that implements EIS you have all the infrastructure in place to talk to libei clients from somewhere else, if need be.
sentences.zh: 如果您有一个实现 EIS 的合成器，那么您就拥有了所有基础设施，可以在需要时与其他地方的 libei 客户端进行对话。

sentences.en: The use-cases for this aren't fully scoped yet (assisitive technologies, virtual keyboards, touchpads, etc?)
sentences.zh: 其用例尚未完全确定（辅助技术、虚拟键盘、触摸板等？）

sentences.en: but the piping is there and ready to be (ab)used .
sentences.zh: 但管道已就位并准备好被（滥用）使用。

blocks.html_en: • since the actual events back and forth don't affect the layers in between, we can now add new events to libei without having to change everything else again.

sentences.en: since the actual events back and forth don't affect the layers in between, we can now add new events to libei without having to change everything else again.
sentences.zh: 由于来回的实际事件不会影响中间的层，因此我们现在可以向 libei 添加新事件，而无需再次更改其他所有内容。

blocks.html_en: Let's look at how this works in practice.

sentences.en: Let's look at how this works in practice.
sentences.zh: 让我们看看这在实践中是如何运作的。

blocks.html_en: The XWayland XTEST use-case

sentences.en: The XWayland XTEST use-case
sentences.zh: XWayland XTEST 用例

blocks.html_en: An example for such a case where we can now abuse the piping is Xwayland support for XTEST. XTEST is the protocol that everyone uses to emulate input under X but in Wayland it's not hooked up to anything so those APIs simply won't work.

sentences.en: An example for such a case where we can now abuse the piping is Xwayland support for XTEST.
sentences.zh: 我们现在可以滥用管道的一个例子是 Xwayland 对 XTEST 的支持。

sentences.en: XTEST is the protocol that everyone uses to emulate input under X but in Wayland it's not hooked up to anything so those APIs simply won't work.
sentences.zh: XTEST 是每个人都用来模拟 X 下输入的协议，但在 Wayland 中它没有连接到任何东西，因此这些 API 根本无法工作。

sentences.zh: 但我们在 Xwayland 能做的就是将 XTEST 转换为 libei 事件并促进门户交互。

sentences.en: This means our stack looks roughly like this:
sentences.zh: 这意味着我们的堆栈大致如下所示：

blocks.html_en: And if said X client uses XTEST to try to emulate devices, Xwayland will ask the Remote Desktop portal for permission and set up the session, then pass the XTEST events on as libei events and voila - your 20 year old X client can send pointer and keyboard events through an XDG Portal without knowing about it (and the user can prohibit this and even gets some information on who is sending events which is not possible with normal XTEST at all). This has now been supported since Xwayland 23.2.0. Compositors don't need extra support for this.

sentences.en: And if said X client uses XTEST to try to emulate devices, Xwayland will ask the Remote Desktop portal for permission and set up the session, then pass the XTEST events on as libei events and voila - your 20 year old X client can send pointer and keyboard events through an XDG Portal without knowing about it (and the user can prohibit this and even gets some information on who is sending events which is not possible with normal XTEST at all).
sentences.zh: 如果所述 X 客户端使用 XTEST 尝试模拟设备，Xwayland 将请求远程桌面门户的许可并设置会话，然后将 XTEST 事件作为 libei 事件传递，瞧 - 您 20 岁的 X 客户端可以在不知情的情况下通过 XDG 门户发送指针和键盘事件（用户可以禁止这一点，甚至获得一些有关谁正在发送事件的信息，这对于普通 XTEST 来说是不可能的）。

sentences.en: This has now been supported since Xwayland 23.2.0.
sentences.zh: 自 Xwayland 23.2.0 以来现已支持此功能。

sentences.en: Compositors don't need extra support for this.
sentences.zh: 合成器不需要额外的支持。

sentences.en: What's next
sentences.zh: 接下来是什么

blocks.html_en: So we have a lot of the plumbing in place, or in another anology: we have a hammer, let's go looking for nails. And right now the nails we can see are sending text, gestures, and tablet support. And those will be the subject of the next few posts.

sentences.en: So we have a lot of the plumbing in place, or in another anology: we have a hammer, let's go looking for nails.
sentences.zh: 所以我们已经准备好了很多管道，或者换个比喻：我们有一把锤子，让我们去找钉子吧。

sentences.en: And right now the nails we can see are sending text, gestures, and tablet support.
sentences.zh: 现在我们可以看到的钉子正在发送文本、手势和平板电脑支持。

sentences.en: And those will be the subject of the next few posts.
sentences.zh: 这些将是接下来几篇文章的主题。

blocks.html_en:
[1]: 6 years ago?! whoah...
[2]: in Remote Desktop's case replacing the DBus emulation APIs which were a Newton's Cradle of wakeups for at least 4 processes per event

sentences.en: [1]: 6 years ago?!
whoah... [2]: in Remote Desktop's case replacing the DBus emulation APIs which were a Newton's Cradle of wakeups for at least 4 processes per event
sentences.zh: 哇... [2]：在远程桌面的情况下，替换 DBus 模拟 API，这是每个事件至少 4 个进程的唤醒的牛顿摇篮

content_html_zh:
事实证明我已经好几年没有谈论鸡蛋了，所以让我们改变一下这一点。 libei 当然是模拟输入的库[1]。

这篇文章主要是回顾一下，因为它已经很长了，并且是我们迄今为止所做的一些工作的简短总结，为即将发布的更多文章做准备。

libei 是逻辑输入事件的传输层，与硬件抽象层 libinput 不同。 在 libinput 的情况下，设备的固件/内核传递位于理智范围内的事件，libinput 尝试理解这些事件，然后我们将它们转换为逻辑事件以供下一层（通常是 Wayland 合成器或 Xorg）使用。 这就是例如如果启用了触摸板点击，“在位置 x1/y1 处触摸，在位置 x1/y2 触摸向上”将转换为按钮单击事件。 或者，如果我们发现这是一次意外的手掌触摸，则可能会一事无成。

libei 纯粹在逻辑级别上工作 - 作为 libei 客户端，您将逻辑事件传递给 EIS（模拟输入服务器）实现（通常是合成器）。 不用猜测，您说单击按钮，EIS 就会单击按钮。 libei 支持“发送者”和“接收者”模式，具体取决于事件是发送到 EIS 实现（输入仿真）还是从 EIS 实现接收（输入捕获）。 libei 是为 Wayland 堆栈设计的，但 Wayland 对客户端或 EIS 实现的要求为零。

libei 设计的核心是 EIS 实现几乎控制一切，它决定哪些设备可供客户端使用、这些设备何时可以发送事件等。就像合成器在物理设备方面负责一样 - 如果合成器确定物理设备不存在，Wayland 客户端就无法从中获取事件。

自最初的提案以来（再次，[1]！） 我们一直很忙，libei 现在是 XDG 远程桌面门户和 XDG 输入捕获的一部分（均自 2023 年中期版本 1.17 起）。 在这两种情况下，门户都是用于协商并就应该发生的事情达成初步协议，然后使用 libei 作为两个进程之间的传输层 [2]。

最近，我们还添加了会话持久性支持，因此您不必允许每个连接上的访问。 实现此功能的大部分工作是由 Jonas Ådahl 完成的，自版本 1.21.0 以来，它现已出现在门户中，并且应该出现在当前或下一版本的主要合成器中。

管道安装

将所有这些都落实到位需要跨堆栈的多个部分进行大量工作。 这并不令人兴奋，就像铺设管道也不是特别令人兴奋一样，但很像普通的管道：一旦安装到位，您就可以改变饮食，而不会再次严重影响每个人。 现在试着把这个比喻从你的脑海中抹去。 不客气。

对于 libei 来说，这意味着三件事：

  • 如果您有一个使用 XDG 门户发送/接收事件的客户端，它们现在将与实现该门户的任何合成器一起使用。 不需要 GNOME/KDE/... 特定的 API。
  • 如果您有一个实现 EIS 的合成器，那么您就拥有了所有基础设施，可以在需要时与其他地方的 libei 客户端进行对话。 其用例尚未完全确定（辅助技术、虚拟键盘、触摸板等？） 但管道已就位并准备好被（滥用）使用。
  • 由于来回的实际事件不会影响中间的层，因此我们现在可以向 libei 添加新事件，而无需再次更改其他所有内容。

让我们看看这在实践中是如何运作的。

XWayland XTEST 用例

我们现在可以滥用管道的一个例子是 Xwayland 对 XTEST 的支持。 XTEST 是每个人都用来模拟 X 下输入的协议，但在 Wayland 中它没有连接到任何东西，因此这些 API 根本无法工作。

但我们在 Xwayland 能做的就是将 XTEST 转换为 libei 事件并促进门户交互。 这意味着我们的堆栈大致如下所示：

    +--------------------+             +------------------+
    | Wayland compositor |---wayland---| Wayland client B |
    +--------------------+\            +------------------+
    | libinput |   EIS   | \_wayland______
    +----------+---------+                \
        |          |           +-------+------------------+
 /dev/input/       +-----------| libei |     XWayland     |
                               +-------+------------------+
                                                |
                                                | XTEST
                                                |
                                         +-----------+
                                         |  X client |
                                         +-----------+

如果所述 X 客户端使用 XTEST 尝试模拟设备，Xwayland 将请求远程桌面门户的许可并设置会话，然后将 XTEST 事件作为 libei 事件传递，瞧 - 您 20 岁的 X 客户端可以在不知情的情况下通过 XDG 门户发送指针和键盘事件（用户可以禁止这一点，甚至获得一些有关谁正在发送事件的信息，这对于普通 XTEST 来说是不可能的）。 自 Xwayland 23.2.0 以来现已支持此功能。 合成器不需要额外的支持。

接下来是什么

所以我们已经准备好了很多管道，或者换个比喻：我们有一把锤子，让我们去找钉子吧。 现在我们可以看到的钉子正在发送文本、手势和平板电脑支持。 这些将是接下来几篇文章的主题。

[1]：6年前？！ 哇... [2]：在远程桌面的情况下，替换 DBus 模拟 API，这是每个事件至少 4 个进程的唤醒的牛顿摇篮
